title: THE POMEGRANATE HOUR · POST
subtitle: Cicadas at Dusk · Vol 5 · Ch II · The High Priestess
notes: Elicia Temple's editing suite — the post-production room for Pomegranate Hour, a six-episode anthology she started in film school in '95 with Hen, Marisol, and Davey. It was supposed to take a year. It is in its twentieth. Two episodes are locked, one has no sound, one has no edit, one has no shoot, and the finale has only a title card and an unused soundstage receipt. The suite is real — built from a public-access station's gear she bought in '12 for $40 when the station closed. The episodes are real, half-real, paper-real, dream-real. The PATCH BAY routes the suite into the footage. Walking from PATCH BAY into HOTEL HALLWAY is stepping into the show. Fourteen spaces — the five-station suite + six episode-spaces accessed through the gateway + three thresholds. Hermetic: no other arcana's people walk in. Their places, sometimes, as locations the show shot at.
ambient_pool: the modem rack from her day job hums under the suite. she bought it for $40 in '12.
the half-eaten pomegranate on the desk has been there since '99. the seeds are dust now.
the master tape spools. it doesn't catch on anything.
davey's cue 14 plays on the monitor at -18db. the draft he marked 'please don't use this one.' she uses it.
the answering machine has seventeen messages. fourteen of them are hen's voice from years she can date by the area code.
the cassette wall hums — twelve hundred conversations, dailies, scratch tracks, voice memos to herself.
the suite knows it is being edited. it has known since '99.

spaces.label: ENTRY HALL
spaces.flavor: the door from the apartment hallway — Marisol walks in here when she comes up from the city.
arrival_lines: the welcome mat is stitched with a pomegranate. you stitched it in '96 and you have not replaced it.
the door takes two tries. it has taken two tries since '03.

spaces.label: ROOF ACCESS
spaces.flavor: the small interior ladder to the roof — where you smoke, when you smoke, which is when an episode stalls.
arrival_lines: the ladder rungs ring like a struck chime. the cicadas above are louder than the modem rack below.
from the roof you can see the river. the river isn't in the show. you wanted it to be.

spaces.label: BACK DOOR
spaces.flavor: the service exit — the route the dailies came in when D'Ambrosio's was a shoot night in '97.
arrival_lines: the back door opens onto a stairwell that smells like the diner two buildings over. it always has.
you brought the Ep. 11 footage up these stairs the night you shot it. you remember how the bag was heavy.

spaces.label: LOUNGE
spaces.flavor: couch, lamp, the magazine from March '97 that none of you ever threw out · where Marisol sleeps when she comes up.
arrival_lines: the couch has Marisol's weight in it from the last visit, and the visit before that.
the lamp's pull-chain has frayed to a single thread. it has been a single thread for nine years.

spaces.label: BOOTH
spaces.flavor: the small ADR room · one chair, one mic at mouth height · where the actors come back to lay sound over picture.
arrival_lines: the booth has its own air. older than the suite.
the mic is at Marisol's mouth height. it was set for her in '96 and you have not moved it.

spaces.label: CONTROL GLASS
spaces.flavor: the window between booth and console, also the monitor wall · the bridge of the suite · everything passes through here.
arrival_lines: the glass has a fingerprint on the booth side. yours. you leaned in once in '97 and never wiped it off.
the monitor wall is at -18 db. four screens, four episodes you can call up at once.

spaces.label: CONSOLE
spaces.flavor: your seat · the scrub bar, faders, the timeline · where the show is actually cut.
arrival_lines: the faders are warm. each one carries a person's voice in your palm.
the timeline is open to Ep. 9 cue 14. it has been open to Ep. 9 cue 14 since you stopped working on it last night.

spaces.label: TAPE LIBRARY
spaces.flavor: twelve hundred cassettes, DATs, Hi8s, U-matics · alphabetised, dated, indexed in your hand · the master copies of every episode + the dailies + the drafts + the locked drawer.
arrival_lines: the library smells like coffee and electrical tape and the inside of a Walkman.
you find a DAT labeled DAVEY · CUE 14 · DRAFT 3 (please don't use this one). it is in his hand.

spaces.label: PATCH BAY
spaces.flavor: the routing wall · forty cables, each labeled in your hand · the gateway. patching INTO an episode here is walking through the door of it.
arrival_lines: the cables are warm where they shouldn't be. they have been warm since '99.
you trace a cable from EPISODE 17 to a jack labeled simply 'self.' you don't remember patching that one.

spaces.label: EP. 3 · HOTEL HALLWAY
spaces.flavor: the long hallway with six doors · soundstage build, '96 · hen's hallway · the only episode that is wholly done.
arrival_lines: the hallway is exactly as you cut it. exactly. it is the only thing in the suite that does not drift.
door 2 is ajar. the woman behind it is sitting on the bed. she nods at you the way she has nodded for twenty years.

spaces.label: EP. 7 · LIGHTHOUSE
spaces.flavor: the cape lighthouse · three october nights '96 · marisol at the top of the tower, mouthing words you can almost hear.
arrival_lines: the lighthouse air has a draft Marisol's coat couldn't keep out. you can see her shoulders set against it.
the keeper is mouthing reel 2's monologue. she has been mouthing it since '96. she does not look impatient.

spaces.label: EP. 9 · WOODS UPSTATE
spaces.flavor: the woods upstate · the entomologist alone · the seventeen-year brood · davey's last cue, unfinished.
arrival_lines: the cicadas are loud the way they were in '96. they were loud in '96 because the brood was emerging. the brood is emerging again this year.
the entomologist sits on a stump. she is listening. she has been listening for twenty years.

spaces.label: EP. 11 · LAST BOOTH
spaces.flavor: d'ambrosio's diner · closed-hours shoot '97 · three sisters in the last booth · the waitress they did not notice.
arrival_lines: the diner is the diner. you shot here for one night, four to ten am, and john gave you the keys.
the waitress is at the booth, refilling cups that have been full since the night you shot. she mouths along to a script you wrote.

spaces.label: EP. 14 · THE LATE BUS
spaces.flavor: cora's bus · the night route between graustark and the next town up · channel 17 stays clear · never filmed.
arrival_lines: the bus exists in your head with the precision of a thing you have driven though you have never driven it.
the late-shift passenger is in seat 14. they have been in seat 14 for twenty years. they would like to ride somewhere.

spaces.label: EP. 17 · THE CONVERSATION
spaces.flavor: the bare soundstage · two chairs, one lamp, eighty-six minutes paid for in march '97 · the conversation that has not happened.
arrival_lines: the stage is dark except for the lamp between the chairs. the chairs are the chairs from your kitchen in college.
the two women are sitting. they look at each other. they do not speak. they have not spoken in twenty years and the not-speaking is not awkward anymore.

episode_states.notes: Each episode-space carries a `state`. The state determines what the space feels like on arrival and what actions are available there. ADVANCE moves an episode up one state. Reversed scenarios start with everything decayed back. The state list is ordered worst → best.

master_reel.label: MASTER REEL
master_reel.notes: Elicia's doom-clock — measures the night's edit work, not the show's overall completeness. Each EDIT / CUT / RECORD action ticks the master reel by 1. At threshold the night is done — no more work, you go to bed. Some scenarios start with the reel half-full (a productive afternoon already in). The reel filling is not a loss; it's the night ending.

ladder.label: the suite is dark. the lamp on the console is the only light.
the first cassette is on. the night has begun.
halfway · the kettle is on its second boil.
the night is making the sound a thing about to run out makes.
the night is over. the lamp on the console is still on. you turn it off.
